### Post by Cirdan7 on 2007-03-23

```
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"radeon"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	VideoRam	131072
	Option	"MonitorLayout"		"LCD, CRT"
	Option	"CRT2Position"		"LeftOf"
	Option	"MetaModes"		"1280x1024-1280x1024"
	Option "MergedXinerama"	"on"
	Option	"MergedNonRectangular"	"true"
	Option	"MergedFB"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"0 Screen"
	Device		"0 Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen        0  "0 Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by djf_jeff on 2007-03-24
Yeah, you must be sure that you have enough memory on your graphic card. With your setup, your graphic must handle 2560x2048 with all the graphic that beryl provide.

### Post by BungaMan on 2007-03-28

### Post by BungaMan on 2007-04-03
I read your post again and realized your setup is a dual monitor with merged framebuffer.  This makes your desktop to have a resolution of 2560x1024.  Beryl makes a texture of your desktop but textures are only supported until a certain size which in most cases is 2048x2048.  This is above your desktop resolution.

Disconnect your second monitor, hit ctrl-alt-backspace and start up beryl.  You'll see that it works now.  Beryl still needs to overcome this texture by splitting it up in several textures when necessary.

---

### Post by freerick on 2007-04-03
I actually don't think it has to do with the textures: I've had the same problem setting up Beryl with three monitors. Your configuration file indicates that you're using Xinerama to emulate a spanned desktop over two monitors. I'm doing the same with three monitors.
If you type 'beryl' on a command prompt, you will see that it attempts to launch and as it does so, outputs messages to the terminal. Those will give you a clue as to what's wrong. My assumption, from the information that I have, is that you will most likely see that the check for "RandR" "failed".

RandR is an extension to X11 that is used to manage in-session resolution switching, as well as resolution changes on-the-fly with physical rotation of the display. For multiple displays this is apparently deemed unnecessary and disabled (it might even cause problems if it were to be forced back on, I don't know).

The bottom line for me was that I was unable to run Beryl as long as I was using Xinerama, at least not in their current configurations. I did, however, successfully run Beryl on dual-monitor with Nvidia TwinView (and the third on Xinerama, which, althought Beryl worked fine, led to some interesting but mostly undesirable side effects). 

My new angle is that we should find a way to patch Beryl and/or X11 and/or RandR to get this fixed. I know that are other people who have implemented a setup such as this, and I'm sure it's not impossible.
Please post any experience you had, good or bad, or any ideas you may have on how to solve this, no matter how stupid you think they sound.

Thanks a lot!
:popcorn:

### Post by BungaMan on 2007-04-04
Freerick, his setup is with merged framebuffer and not xinerama.  Trust me.  And with merged framebuffer the texture size limit does come into picture.
